apps.description: A native terminal for Ainkrad — command blocks, theme-matched colors, SwiftTerm-backed.
apps.author: Ahmed M. Elhalaby
apps.longDescription:
Terminal brings a full, native command line into your Ainkrad workspace — built on a SwiftTerm-backed emulator, not a web view.

• Command blocks — each command and its output is a distinct, scrollable block you can navigate, re-run, and reason about, instead of one endless stream.
• Theme-matched — colors are driven by the host's DesignTokens, so the terminal always matches your active Ainkrad theme; switch themes and it restyles instantly without losing scrollback.
• Real emulation — a proper PTY underneath: interactive programs, colors, resizing, and control sequences all work as expected.
• Fits the workspace — open it as a floating island, resize freely, and run several alongside your other apps.

Designed to feel like part of the OS surface — seamless chrome, no separator lines, traffic-lights inside the window.

apps.description: A full HUD Git IDE for Ainkrad — changes, history, branches, PRs, issues, worktrees, and an interactive commit graph.
apps.author: Ahmed M. Elhalaby
apps.longDescription:
Git Mage is a complete Git workspace for Ainkrad, wrapped in a gaming-HUD interface that feels like part of the OS.

Workspace
• Multi-repo library — add as many repositories as you like and switch between them (GitHub-Desktop style), each remembering its own state.
• Three-pane shell — a nav rail into Changes, History, Stashes, Pull Requests, Issues, Worktrees and Advanced, with full-surface repo and branch management overlays.

Everyday Git
• Changes — stage/unstage/discard by file or all at once, with a live commit box.
• History — paginated commit log that loads as you scroll, with a git-graph rail.
• Branches & Stashes — create, checkout, delete; stash push/pop/apply/drop with per-stash diffs.

Review
• GitHub-style diff viewer — syntax highlighting, line-number gutter, background-only add/remove tints, and collapsible per-file diffs shared across Changes, History, Stashes and PRs.
• Interactive worktree commit graph — a real multi-lane branch/merge graph; click any commit to inspect its diff.

GitHub
• Pull Requests & Issues — search, label filters and pagination via the GitHub API; Conversation / Commits / Files tabs; markdown descriptions and comments; review, merge, comment, close/reopen, labels and assignees.

Advanced
• Cherry-pick, revert, reset, rebase and tag management — contextual to the commit you select, with confirm-gated destructive ops.

Made yours
• Configurable keyboard shortcuts for every action and area, and a configurable typography theme (font family + size) applied live across the whole app.
